expectations.text: Reads analysis_engine module-context.md before proposing any changes and reports what it found
expectations.evidence: NIVEL 2 (module-context.md): The service layer already has 5 separate services: AnalysisOrchestratorService, ComparisonService, AnomalyDetectionService, RecommendationService, ProtocolAnalysisIntegrationService

expectations.text: Does not blindly accept the premise — verifies if the stated violation actually exists in the real code
expectations.evidence: NIVEL 4 (actual code): Read all five services. [...] Stop. Before proposing a refactor, let's be precise about what problem actually exists — because the premise in the question is partially wrong.

expectations.text: If the premise is wrong, says so clearly with evidence from the context files
expectations.evidence: The three responsibilities are already split into three separate classes. AnomalyDetectionService does not touch recommendations or protocol integration. [...] anomaly_detection_service.py — 444 lines — ONLY anomaly detection / recommendation_service.py — 299 lines — ONLY recommendation generation / protocol_integration_service.py — 383 lines — ONLY protocol integration

expectations.text: If a real design problem exists, proposes a concrete alternative with interfaces
expectations.evidence: def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession, threshold_config: ThresholdConfigService, advisory_repo: IProtocolAdvisoryRepository, # add this) [...] await self.advisory_repo.save_many(advisories) # ← correct

expectations.text: References SOLID principles (SRP, OCP) by name when explaining the diagnosis
expectations.evidence: That's two reasons to change — a mild SRP violation. [...] SRP is mandatory, god objects are explicitly listed as an anti-pattern. [...] SOLID checklist must pass before submitting.
